summary: Justin teaches a systematic framework for identifying good multifamily properties through location assessment, people/processes evaluation, and competitive comp analysis. He emphasizes that location (neighborhood feel, single-family adjacency, path of progress) cannot be changed and is foundational, while people and processes can be improved through operational value-add.
takeaways: Drive the property at multiple times (morning, midday, evening, weekday, weekend) to assess neighborhood dynamics
Target properties abutting single-family home neighborhoods, not isolated in commercial/industrial zones
Count bars on windows as a crime indicator during initial property assessment
Seek unit mix of 55%+ two-bedrooms (ideally more two-bedrooms than anything else, minimum 50-50 ratio)
Evaluate comps in person - 'get boots on the ground, walk the neighborhoods'
Property management staff, maintenance staff, and tenant base demographics make up 'the people' evaluation pillar
Expect to replace property management staff and update processes in 9 out of 10 acquisitions for operational value-add

skill_candidates.change: Systematize property evaluation with 4-pillar framework (Location, People, Processes, Comps) rather than ad-hoc assessment
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin teaches specific checklist: location feel/adjacency/path-of-progress, people/PM-staff/tenant-demo assessment, process documentation (PM software, work order handling, reporting cadence), and competitive comp analysis

skill_candidates.change: Conduct in-person neighborhood reconnaissance at multiple time periods before underwriting
skill_candidates.evidence: 'We'll drive down the street... We'll go in the morning. We'll go in the midday. We'll go in the evening. We'll go on the weekday and weekend' - emphasizes personal observation over computer-based data

skill_candidates.change: Apply unit-mix targeting rule of 55%+ two-bedrooms minimum 50-50 as initial screening filter
skill_candidates.evidence: 'We'd like to see 55 plus percent two-bedrooms in the complex... we can push the delta on a value add typically greater on a two bedroom'

title: Lesson 2: How To Select Properties
